# EnsembleRateThroughTime { #tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime }

```python
tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime(members, split_times)
```

Rate through time across an ARG ensemble — one :class:`RateThroughTime` per member, with a
**confidence interval on the split time** from the ensemble spread.

Returned by :meth:`tspaint.Painting.rate_through_time` when the painting was built from an
ensemble of tree sequences (e.g. SINGER posterior samples): each member is dated on the shared
fit, and the per-member split-time estimates become an interval — the ARG-uncertainty band on
*when* the ancestries diverged. For ``K > 2`` use the per-pair methods (:meth:`pair_split_times`
/ :meth:`pair_split_time_ci`); the scalar :meth:`split_time` / :meth:`split_time_ci` are
2-state-only.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**members**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[RateThroughTime](`tspaint.dating.em.RateThroughTime`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per-member directional rate profiles, on a shared time grid (so they average).

<code>[**split_times**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``(M,)`` per-member scalar split-time estimates (generations ago) for a 2-state fit; all ``nan`` for ``K > 2`` (use :meth:`pair_split_times`). Build with :meth:`from_members`.

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [from_members](#tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.from_members) | Build an ensemble from per-member :class:`RateThroughTime` profiles. |
| [pair_split_time_ci](#tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.pair_split_time_ci) | Per-**pair** percentile confidence interval ``(lo, hi)`` on the split time (any ``K``). |
| [pair_split_times](#tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.pair_split_times) | Per-**pair** point-estimate split time over members (works for any ``K``). |
| [plot](#tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.plot) | Per-member rate curves (faint) + the ensemble mean + the per-pair split-time CI band. |
| [split_time](#tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.split_time) | Point estimate of the split time over members (``"median"`` or ``"mean"``; 2-state only). |
| [split_time_ci](#tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.split_time_ci) | Percentile confidence interval ``(lo, hi)`` on the split time from the ensemble spread |

### from_members { #tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.from_members }

```python
tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.from_members(members)
```

Build an ensemble from per-member :class:`RateThroughTime` profiles.

Populates :attr:`split_times` with each member's scalar split time for a 2-state fit
(``nan`` per member for ``K > 2`` — use :meth:`pair_split_times` there).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**members**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[iterable](`iterable`)\[[RateThroughTime](`tspaint.dating.em.RateThroughTime`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per-member directional rate profiles, sharing one time grid.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[EnsembleRateThroughTime](`tspaint.dating.em.EnsembleRateThroughTime`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The assembled ensemble.

### pair_split_time_ci { #tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.pair_split_time_ci }

```python
tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.pair_split_time_ci(level=0.95)
```

Per-**pair** percentile confidence interval ``(lo, hi)`` on the split time (any ``K``).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**level**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.95]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Central mass of the interval. Default ``0.95``.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[int](`int`), [int](`int`)\], [tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``{(m, n): (lo, hi)}`` (generations ago), ``(nan, nan)`` where no member has a finite value.

### pair_split_times { #tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.pair_split_times }

```python
tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.pair_split_times(statistic='median')
```

Per-**pair** point-estimate split time over members (works for any ``K``).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**statistic**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [\'median\']{.parameter-default}</code>

:   ``"median"`` (default) or ``"mean"`` over the finite per-member estimates of each pair.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[int](`int`), [int](`int`)\], [float](`float`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``{(m, n): split_time}`` (generations ago), ``nan`` where no member has a finite value.

### plot { #tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.plot }

```python
tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.plot(
    ax=None,
    scale=1.0,
    level=0.95,
    colors=None,
    facet=None,
    logy=False,
)
```

Per-member rate curves (faint) + the ensemble mean + the per-pair split-time CI band.

Every directional rate is drawn, each **unordered** ancestry pair in one colour with
``q_{mn}`` (``m<n``) solid and ``q_{nm}`` dashed. Because the pairs can sit on very
different scales, ``K > 2`` **facets by default** — one subplot per pair, each with its own
y-axis and its own split-time CI band — while ``K = 2`` stays a single overlaid axis.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**ax**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [matplotlib.axes.Axes or array of Axes]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Axes to draw on. Default ``None`` — a new figure (single axes, or a column of ``len(pairs)`` axes when faceting).

<code>[**scale**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Multiply the plotted rates (e.g. pass ``Ne`` to show ``rate × N``). Default ``1.0``.

<code>[**level**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.95]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Confidence level for the shaded split-time band (:meth:`pair_split_time_ci`). Default ``0.95``.

<code>[**colors**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Per-pair colour override. Default ``None`` — the default Matplotlib colour cycle.

<code>[**facet**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[bool](`bool`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   One subplot per unordered pair vs. a single overlaid axis. Default ``None`` — facet when ``K > 2``, overlay when ``K == 2``.

<code>[**logy**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[bool](`bool`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [False]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Use a log y-axis. Default ``False``.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[matplotlib](`matplotlib`).[axes](`matplotlib.axes`).[Axes](`matplotlib.axes.Axes`) or [list](`list`)\[[matplotlib](`matplotlib`).[axes](`matplotlib.axes`).[Axes](`matplotlib.axes.Axes`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The single axes (overlay) or the list of per-pair axes (faceted).

### split_time { #tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.split_time }

```python
tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.split_time(statistic='median')
```

Point estimate of the split time over members (``"median"`` or ``"mean"``; 2-state only).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**statistic**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [\'median\']{.parameter-default}</code>

:   ``"median"`` (default) or ``"mean"`` over the finite per-member split-time estimates.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The point-estimate split time (generations ago), or ``nan`` if no member has a finite estimate.

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

<code>[:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[ValueError](`ValueError`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   If ``K != 2``. Use :meth:`pair_split_times` for a per-pair dict.

### split_time_ci { #tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.split_time_ci }

```python
tspaint.EnsembleRateThroughTime.split_time_ci(level=0.95)
```

Percentile confidence interval ``(lo, hi)`` on the split time from the ensemble spread
(2-state only).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**level**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.95]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Central mass of the interval (e.g. ``0.95`` for a 95% interval). Default ``0.95``.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [tuple of float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``(lo, hi)`` percentile interval on the split time (generations ago), or ``(nan, nan)`` if no member has a finite estimate.

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

<code>[:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[ValueError](`ValueError`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   If ``K != 2``. Use :meth:`pair_split_time_ci` for a per-pair dict.
